titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Bolivia ( Flack ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Bolivia ( Flack ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 876. Minister National EconomyEduardo Saenz García. yesterday informed Embassy he would submit request thru FonOff for additional 18,000 tons wheat flour, 30,000 tons wheat, delivery at rate of 8,000 tons monthly beginning October. He expressed concern over impending food shortage which he attributes directly to non-arrivals from Argentina.Telegram 887, September 9, 11 a.m., from La Paz, reported that Argentine foods were beginning to enter Bolivia (824.61311/9–946). Suplies of beef vegetable oils and dairy products considered dangerously low, all of which normally obtained from Argentina. YPFB oil camps facing food crisis due same cause and have requested Embassy assistance obtain shipment approximately 645 tons miscellaneous foodstuffs.

Newspaper reports deny existence Argentine embargo but Embassy believes Argentine pressure being exerted for political purposes (possibly related to pending political asylum matter). Further Peruvian AmbassadorEduardo Garland Roel. told me last night that negotiations with his country regarding supplying sugar and petroleum interrupted until asylum matterFor documentation on the revolution in Bolivia, see pp. 340 ff. settled. Bolivian FonOff asked GalbraithWillard Galbraith, First Secretary of Embassy. orally yesterday that flour and wheat shipments from United States be urgently expedited. SubsecretaryJulio Alvarado. also stated orally he had complained to Argentine ChargéCarlos Torres Gigena. yesterday afternoon about non-arrival foodstuffs from Argentina for past 33 days regardless of statements of Argentine high officials. Chargé expressed personal sympathy and said he would inform his Government. Subsecretary also told Chargé FonOff contemplating
public statement of food situation caused by Argentine failure to permit exports if no food arrives by September 9.
